data.epi_content:
# Evan

There's a student named Evan who's at an age where it's hard to tell whether to call him a boy or a young man.

He possessed a rather sturdy body compared to his peers and a mind far more brilliant than others beyond his age group.

And when he entered a room and stepped into the bathroom, his first thought was to curse his own well-functioning brain.

His second thought was that today would probably be the most terrible day of his life.

Evan recalls.

A girl is dead.

The girl he had a major argument with is dead.

The girl who said family roots and honor were trivial, worthless, and useless is dead.

The girl who was just talking normally with Vivian and him is dead.

That girl who would give a slightly blurry smile, yet somehow seemed to be mocking him, making him feel uncomfortable.

The foolish girl who blew her head off for the first time, or more precisely, should have blown her neck off, is lying there dying.

She'll die soon, and he doesn't know what will happen after that, but anyway.

Evan recalled.

A little, no. Quite, no, that's not it either.

Very shocking, just indescribable.

Anyway, when something like this happens, you can't tell if it's reality or not.

Why on earth?

Why did this happen?

Just moments ago, she seemed to be smiling and talking normally.

Maybe she wasn't really okay.

Still, she was alive.

She looked a bit dejected, like someone who had given up.

I thought she was just giving up on trying to get between Vivian and me.

I didn't know she was giving up on life.

You said I tore you apart with words because I hated you.

But I don't hate you.

And you, while liking me too, slashed at me with just strings of words.

Maybe, as you said, we shouldn't have fought.

Because we know too much about each other, because we can guess what's in each other's hearts, because we know too well what wounds the other has and what words will hurt them.

Anyway, if someone were to ask how this middling young man named Evan came to witness the girl's death.

Three men and Lydia, was it? Erica's minion who used to help her bully Vivian came looking for him.

Perhaps they were just desperately looking for anyone who could use healing magic and happened to grab him.

With a horrified face, they ran somewhere, and upon seeing his face, asked him to follow them.

At first, he wanted to refuse, but seeing the urgency or desperation on their faces, he decided to follow.

A stiff but shocked expression is itself quite persuasive.

They were somewhat rough, muscular men with swords at their waists, suggesting they were knights, or rather, officer cadets as they're called these days.

The era of knights who rode horses, wore armor, and roamed battlefields directly had completely ended.

Anyway, those two were muttering about getting involved in something unnecessary, saying they followed because Lydia told them to, and now what trouble had they gotten into.

The place they brought Evan to was Erica's room.

As he opened the door and stepped in, he heard a splashing sound.

Looking down, he saw slightly reddish water filling the room.

At the same time, a terrible fishy smell.

He wanted to deny it, but he realized it was the smell of blood.

He had learned what the red liquid from a human body smelled like when he was very young.
data.epi_content2:
Drip. Drip. Drizzle.

Water was overflowing from the bathtub.

And whenever whatever was in the bathtub, once breathing, convulsed, that red water spilled over.

"...Ah."

Unnaturally stiff, outstretched legs.

White clothes soaked in water, revealing bare skin.

A wound on the left wrist, bleeding red as if paint were being sprinkled.

A blade with something like sticky black mucus on it, visible as he slightly turned his head.

In the water, a pistol with ivory grip and gold decorations that Erica had once proudly said was a gift from her father.

Looking at her open mouth, there was a large hole behind it.

And behind that, blood had burst red, as if flour had been gathered and thrown.

Unnaturally floating, dead fish-like eyes as usual.

Her cheeks, already pale and bloodless, now seemed to have no color at all.

He immediately raised his right hand, placed his fingers on her, and used detection magic to sense if there was even a faint pulse.

And at that point, Erica was still alive.

"Hey, Erica."

He wrapped light healing magic around his hand and placed it on her wrist.

But the magic didn't work.

"Open your eyes. Say something. You can even say you wish someone like me would die. Just open your..."

There were two reasons why it didn't work.

One was that Erica was already dead, but wanting to believe she was still alive, he hallucinated a faint pulse in her wrist.

The other was that Erica had already let go of all desire to live.

The wound was so painful that she didn't even want it to close.

Nevertheless, he continued using healing magic.

Then her gaze moved slightly before stopping completely.

The mouth of the girl who used to speak harshly to the boy and couldn't even make expressions was smiling brightly.

"Ugh... stop smiling."

Evan's legs gave out, and he fell backward, landing on his bottom.

Still, he couldn't take his eyes off Erica's face.

Empty eyes and smiling mouth, dark circles, pale skin, and the red blood blooming behind.

That appearance showed that Erica would never cry in front of him again.

It indicated that she would never receive an apple or eat bread together with him while smiling again.

It stated that she would never speak insulting words to the middling young man, hurting each other and getting angry again.

It revealed that she would never again make that uniquely empty expression in front of Evan, who always pretended not to notice no matter how much she craved love.

The boy thought he knew well.

How could he not know such things when he had spent so much time with the girl?

And he had never once considered the possibility of the girl choosing death.

He knew that the wounds in her mouth were clearly burns from direct contact with fire, not just hot water.

He knew that she had always hated pain and would whine for healing magic immediately.

Always immersed in the illusion of a normal reality, he failed to notice how things around him were changing because of the ink of love spilled over it.

Until they came to the Academy, until before their relationship soured, until before hearing the news that Erica's brother, whose head would have already fallen off, had died, she had been a bright and cheerful child who never seemed to tire, and thus was more charming than anyone else.

"I wish you wouldn't die... why. No. I don't know what to say. Ah. Ugh."

He placed his hand on her left chest.
data.epi_content3:
Erica might have blushed and felt embarrassed.

Moreover, her white clothes were soaked, making her almost naked.

Still, if the boy had said he had no choice but to use healing magic, she would have nodded and endured the embarrassment.

But that Erica no longer existed in this world.

Her heart had stopped.

All strength drained from his body.

Had he exhausted all his magical power?

He would gladly never use magic again if it meant bringing Erica back to life.

Setting aside their relationship as man and woman, Erica was the only friend he had ever opened his heart to.

Perhaps what he did was incredibly cruel... he knew.

That Erica's feelings toward him were not friendship but something in a slightly different direction.

Perhaps he was drawn to Vivian because she gave him a fresh new feeling without the familiar feeling he got from Erica.

"Thinking about this, I really am trash..."

Or am I trash?

Because all this time, I never tried to find out what Erica was doing, how she was living, what she was thinking, and just ran away.

Would this child named Lydia have treated well a child from an already collapsed family?

Coming to a girl's room alone at this hour with two strong men doesn't seem very wholesome.

Evan thinks about these things.

And after recalling the words and actions he had directed at Erica, he sighs and sits down on the blood-diluted water, thinking that he wasn't much different.

The sun had already set.

He looks around Erica's room.

There was nothing in the girl's room.

Things you'd expect in a girl's room, furniture, not even a single picture.

When he had visited before, it was a room full of characteristics that anyone would recognize as Erica's room.

"Was she living in a place like this?"

There was no answer.

Those who had brought Evan to this room had long since left.

Only the sound of water splashing and overflowing could be heard.

"I'm sorry."

He buried his head in his knees and muttered empty words that no one would hear.

"I'm sorry."

If a noble lowers his head and apologizes, many things in the world can be resolved.

But how could an inanimate corpse understand any words spoken to it?

In the end, the boy's apology was nothing but an empty echo with no one to hear it.

That's why Evan recalled.

That he should have apologized.

But since all possibilities of apologizing had already disappeared, he was just frustrated.

"I'm... sorry. But this is a bit too..."

He was about to say "harsh" but swallowed the words.

His mouth felt more bitter than if he had drunk the strong coffee Erica used to enjoy.

So the words he uttered were just three syllables appropriate for the situation.

"I'm sorry."
